problem_statement: A municipal reminder service stopped scheduling collection-day notices for 1,842 addresses after the weekly geographic routing import. The web portal still shows valid addresses and the scheduler remains healthy for most districts. Diagnose the first broken handoff and a durable correction using retained import artifacts and offline copies; do not send test notices to residents.

observations.statement: The delivery sink recorded no messages for 1,842 eligible addresses in districts 4 and 7, while aggregate scheduler throughput remained within 2 percent of its prior-week range.
The routing consumer emitted an unmatched-route result for correlation import-2026w27-d4 even though the producer envelope was accepted and its route field rendered visually as RTE-41.
observations.provenance: consumer trace joined to import envelope

observations.statement: Byte-level inspection of the retained producer artifact shows U+2011 between RTE and 41 for every missed district-4 record; the reference route table uses U+002D at that position.
observations.provenance: retained GIS export and route-table snapshot

observations.statement: In an isolated database copy, changing only U+2011 to U+002D in the retained artifact restored all 1,842 route joins under the deployed consumer and unchanged settings.
Replaying the unmodified retained artifact in the same isolated database reproduced all 1,842 misses, excluding elapsed queue load and resident-message delivery from the first failure.
Districts 1, 2, 3, 5, and 6 used U+002D route identifiers and completed the same producer-consumer-sink path on the same service revisions.
The route table snapshot contains all referenced district-4 and district-7 routes, and direct lookup by their stored U+002D identifiers succeeds.
The import schema accepted both characters because its route field permits general Unicode punctuation; no schema or application revision changed during the affected week.

Notification templates, district schedules, and sender credentials have unchanged fingerprints, and a synthetic notice built from an already matched control address reaches the non-delivery capture sink.
